title: DNS Failure Detector
description:
Given the output of multiple `dig` lookups concatenated together, identify which hostnames failed.

Each lookup block starts with a line containing `<<>> DiG` followed by the queried hostname. A lookup failed if its header line contains `status: SERVFAIL` or `status: NXDOMAIN`.

Print one failed hostname per line in the order they appear. Return empty output if all succeeded.
constraints: Each query block starts with a line matching `<<>> DiG`
The hostname is the last whitespace-separated token on that line
A failure is indicated by 'status: SERVFAIL' or 'status: NXDOMAIN' in the header line
Output one hostname per line in order of appearance

examples.input:
; <<>> DiG 9.16 <<>> api.example.com
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NOERROR, id: 1
;; ANSWER SECTION:
api.example.com. 300 IN A 10.0.0.1

; <<>> DiG 9.16 <<>> db.example.com
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: SERVFAIL, id: 2
;; QUESTION SECTION:
;db.example.com. IN A

starter_code.bash:
#!/bin/bash
# Read dig output from stdin
# Print each failed hostname (SERVFAIL or NXDOMAIN)
current_host=""
while IFS= read -r line; do

test_cases.input:
; <<>> DiG 9.16 <<>> api.example.com
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NOERROR, id: 1
;; ANSWER SECTION:
api.example.com. 300 IN A 10.0.0.1

; <<>> DiG 9.16 <<>> db.example.com
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: SERVFAIL, id: 2
;; QUESTION SECTION:
;db.example.com. IN A

; <<>> DiG 9.16 <<>> a.example.com
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NOERROR, id: 1
;; ANSWER SECTION:
a.example.com. 60 IN A 10.0.0.1

; <<>> DiG 9.16 <<>> ok.example.com
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NOERROR, id: 1
;; ANSWER SECTION:
ok.example.com. 300 IN A 1.2.3.4

; <<>> DiG 9.16 <<>> dead.example.com
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: SERVFAIL, id: 2
;; QUESTION SECTION:
;dead.example.com. IN A

; <<>> DiG 9.16 <<>> ghost.example.com
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NXDOMAIN, id: 3
;; QUESTION SECTION:
;ghost.example.com. IN A
